mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail and housing fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC above 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained job creation, reinforcing consumer spending capacity and equity market confidence to produce positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5 percent annually forces central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPI growth exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales validates synchronized macroeconomic expansion, reducing recession probability and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production indices during late-cycle phases signal capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering profit margin compression and immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes during late-cycle phases produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases.
